articles.title: ‘To the people of Boston, thank you’: Jaylen Brown’s statement after reportedly being traded by the Celtics
articles.description:
"I’m still processing how this all went down. I’m excited and disappointed at the same time."

The post ‘To the people of Boston, thank you’: Jaylen Brown’s statement after reportedly being traded by the Celtics appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jaylen Brown expresses mixed emotions after being traded from the Celtics.
Brown thanked Boston fans in a statement following the trade announcement.
The trade may impact Celtics' performance and fan engagement in the upcoming season.

articles.title: Review & setlist: It was 100 degrees in Boston, and Goose was on fire
articles.description:
The Connecticut jam band delivered an incendiary show at Leader Bank Pavilion Wednesday night.

The post Review & setlist: It was 100 degrees in Boston, and Goose was on fire appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Goose performed a high-energy concert at Leader Bank Pavilion on July 1, 2026.
The temperature reached 100 degrees, affecting concertgoers' comfort and attendance.
Local businesses benefited from increased foot traffic during the popular event.

articles.title: Everything to know about July 4 in Boston (including how to watch the fireworks)
articles.description:
From the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular (and how to watch on TV or in person) to the reading of the Declaration of Independence, here's your guide to Fourth of July weekend in Boston.

The post Everything to know about July 4 in Boston (including how to watch the fireworks) appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Celebrate July 4 with the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular at the Charles River Esplanade.
Watch the fireworks live on July 4, starting at 10:30 PM, or on local TV.
Experience the historic reading of the Declaration of Independence during the festivities.

articles.title: In new podcast, former North Andover police officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons calls first judge ‘evil’ and recounts being shot
articles.description:
Fitzsimmons was shot by a colleague last year and eventually acquitted of an assault charge.

The post In new podcast, former North Andover police officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons calls first judge ‘evil’ and recounts being shot appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover officer, recounts being shot by a colleague last year.
Fitzsimmons was acquitted of an assault charge related to the incident.
Her podcast criticizes the judicial system, impacting local perceptions of law enforcement accountability.
